We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to address the issues brought herein and to:

Existence of unreconciled balance between General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office

a) Direct the GSO and the Accounting Unit to identify the details of the variance through the List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) prepared after the physical inventory to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts as part of the one-time cleansing;

b) Direct the GSO and Accounting Unit to reconcile all records and reports pertaining to PPE after the one-time cleansing and establish the correct balances of the PPE account to be recorded in the books of accounts; and

c) Direct the same to review all documents and reports before submission to the Office of the Audit Team to ensure that the details presented thereon are accurate and complete.

Erroneous and unreliable computation of Accumulated Depreciation

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to further evaluate the cause of the under provision of accumulated depreciation (Annex B). Thereafter, if proven to have been erroneously computed, prepare appropriate adjusting entry taking into consideration the proper treatment of prior period adjustments.

b) Henceforth, ensure uniform and proper application of depreciation for all the PPE, follow pertinent guidelines in the recognition of asset, and prepare PPE Lapsing Schedule for monitoring purposes.

We recommended to the management to:

a) Direct the GSO and the Accounting Unit to conduct regular reconciliation of semi-expendable properties and establish the correct balances of the Inventory and Expense account to be recorded in the books of accounts at year-end through proper adjusting entry taking into consideration the proper treatment of prior period adjustments.

b) Establish controls and accountability for semi-expendable properties such as maintenance of Semi-Expendable Property Card (SPC), Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card (SPLC), Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RegSPI) and other prescribed forms under COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 as maybe applicable such as Inventory Transfer Report (ITR), Receipt of Returned Semi-Expendable Property (RRSP), and Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RSPI); and

c) Assign accountable personnel/office for inventory management to ensure ongoing compliance, accurate tracking, and proper valuation of semi-expendable properties in subsequent reporting period.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the following actions be undertaken by management:
Immediately conduct a thorough reconciliation of the Cashbooks- Cash In Bank and the SL and take up the adjustments to reconcile both books.
After the cashbook and SL are reconciled, both books shall take up the adjustments to record the identified reconciling items between the two books and the bank statements.
Ensure that, at least, a quarterly reconciliation between the Accountant and the Treasurer shall be practiced.
If additional manpower cannot be pursued, the preparation of the BRS have to be delegated to a capable and knowledgeable personnel in the Accounting Office, who will also be tasked to reconcile with the Treasurer’s Cashbooks. The preparation of the BRS shall be done on a timely manner so that any reconciling items found therein can be adjusted immediately.
Update the BRS of the General Fund PPSB # 8-002366-231, since this account contains the bulk of the transactions of the LGU.

Recommendation:
Require the Liquidating Officer to strictly implement the provision of Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382.   Require all revenue collectors to remit on a daily basis and thoroughly check as to the completeness of the remittance made.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the following actions be undertaken by management immediately:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and verify the identified reconciling items in the initial reconciliation made during the cash examination. After analysis and verification, effect the necessary adjustment in the corresponding books of accounts.
Any adjustments that will be made both in the cashbook and in the books of accounts shall be properly supported and explained.
Designate additional manpower to delegate the function of the Liquidating Officer, so that the Municipal Treasurer can undertake reconciliation of the transactions after June 30, 2017.
Ensure monthly reconciliation of balances to avoid any unaccounted collections and deposits and to aid in the discovery of any unremitted collections.
Immediately deposit any balances in the Cash in Treasury accounts to avoid shortages.
Ensure that every SCD prepared by Municipal Treasurer be supported with Validated Deposit Slips corresponding to the amount of the SCD.

Recommendation:
Require the Local Chief Executive to legally designate a Special Disbursing Officer (SDOs) out of these officers and employees for other legally authorized purposes; Once designated, the officers and employees should be properly and sufficiently bonded and should maintain distinct and separate Official Cashbooks for handy monitoring and easy validation of their spending. For remaining unliquidated cash advances, we further recommended that these outstanding balances be settled and liquidated immediately.

Recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the entries of these transactions to the Construction-in-Progress account and secure Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) or any similar documents like certification that would attest to the total completion of the project before recognizing and reporting the total project cost to its corresponding asset account; Ensure coherence in the recording of these specific PPE accounts by firstly, determining the intended purpose of the project being constructed and secondly, properly match it and account it based on the recently released PPSAS definition of account titles and other pertinent COA rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
Instruct the Supply Officer to conduct inventory of the abovementioned items which should be done annually and reclassify as inventory those that remain unutilized with the Inventory report as the basis; End users of the inventory items should submit Utilization Schedules or Consumption Reports to completely substantiate and fully justify recognition of expense on these goods.
Thus, require the Accountant to account purchase of supplies and materials, whether they are consumed or not within the accounting period, and record as debit to Inventory Account. For check and balance, the Property and Supply Office shall maintain Stock cards (SC) for inventories. The balance in quantity per SC should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the Accounting Units.

Recommendation:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to put this improper practice to a halt by cautiously checking every single Purchase Request received; Management should pass a memorandum informing all departments (usually the requisitioning parties) to exclude brand names in devising Purchase Requests in times when the necessity to acquire goods like IT equipment will arise.

Recommendation:
Management should properly observe the correct procedure of Negotiated Procurement – Small Value Procurement by preparing Requests for Quotation from a minimum of three (3) suppliers in purchasing commodities and must stop buying directly from LIBEMCO or MOFE’s Pastries & Catering Services unless they quote the lowest cost among other sellers; Management should bear in mind that the Approved Budget in the Purchase Request is the threshold in every procurement and the amount of its corresponding Purchase Order must not exceed or go beyond it.

The Municipal Accountant should make further analysis on the above cited reconciling items and gather data to support its adjustments; Adjust promptly all stale checks and maintain records and documents of those transactions to support subsequent claims for payment; Update the BRS of the GF-EE PPSB 0008-004260-271, TF-LBP PAMANA 3532-0033-17, GF PPSB account 0008-002366-231 and GF LBP account 3532-0003-26; Transfer the remaining Cash In Bank balances in the General Fund totaling P12,900.53 to the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Some reconciling items were already taken up in the books in CY 2017
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to update the BRS thus adjustments cannot be taken up immediately.

Direct the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the perfected contracts and purchase orders together with all the supporting documents within the prescribed timeframe to enable us to conduct timely review and evaluation.
